rationale: In the theoretical background, the cited study is linked to a specific claim that self-focused consumers rely more on preexisting attitudes and beliefs in choice. The citing paper uses this as part of the prior marketing evidence that self-focus affects consumer decision processes, then positions its own contribution as examining affective-feeling reliance, which prior work had not addressed. This is more than a passing mention, even though there is no direct method reuse from the cited paper.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Goukens et al. (2009) as conceptual prior work showing that private self-focus changes how consumers choose. It uses that result to ground and motivate its own theory about self-focus increasing reliance on affective feelings.
